creator: kib@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Jason Unovitch from comment #5)
What does 'M' mean in your kernel revision ? What changes did you apply ?

From the core at the reference above, in kgdb do 'frame 12\nprint *mp' and post the result.

There were some changes to the vnode handling in the timeframe of recent two weeks, but your problem is about the state of the struct mount, and not vnode.
